rootCauseClusters.name: Unguarded Public API
rootCauseClusters.description: The webhook is effectively public: auth token is embedded in client JS, no rate limiting exists, and no input size limits are enforced. These three independent gaps converge on enabling cost abuse and spam flooding.

rootCauseClusters.name: Fail-Open Prioritizes Availability Over Correctness
rootCauseClusters.description: AI failure defaults spam_score to 0 (all messages pass as legitimate). Google Sheets placeholder fails silently. Build Warnings tracks Slack/Email failures but not Sheets failures. The resilience pattern is internally consistent but creates silent correctness gaps.

rootCauseClusters.name: Fragile String-Based Coupling
rootCauseClusters.description: n8n Code nodes reference other nodes by string name. validation.js globals depend on script load order. These are inherent n8n/browser constraints, not design flaws, but create fragile integration points.

findings.description: Auth token 'demo-token-2026' embedded in client-side JS. Visible to any page visitor. Documented as accepted risk in SECURITY.md but means headerAuth provides no real abuse protection.
findings.suggestion: For production: proxy form submissions through a backend that holds the token server-side. For demo: acceptable as-is with the documented caveat.

findings.description: No rate limiting at any layer. Each request triggers an OpenAI API call ($0.01-0.03). With retryOnFail x3, worst case is 3x cost per request. 1000 automated requests = $30-90 in minutes. Documented in SECURITY.md with 3 mitigation options but none implemented.
findings.suggestion: Add n8n env vars N8N_RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=true + N8N_RATE_LIMIT_REQUESTS_PER_MINUTE=30 to .env as minimum protection.

findings.description: No maxlength on any HTML input/textarea. Server-side validation only checks notEmpty, not length. A 50KB message passes validation and goes to OpenAI, consuming disproportionate tokens and potentially causing 30s+ processing time.
findings.suggestion: Add maxlength='500' to message textarea, maxlength='100' to other fields. Add length check in Validate Fields node.

findings.description: User input (name, email, subject, message) is string-concatenated directly into the OpenAI prompt. An attacker can craft a message that overrides classification instructions, forcing spam_score: 0 to bypass detection, or injecting arbitrary content into draft_response that gets forwarded to Slack/Email.
findings.suggestion: Escape user input or use OpenAI structured outputs / function calling to separate instructions from data. At minimum, add a disclaimer note that AI classification is advisory only.

findings.description: AI Fallback Handler defaults spam_score to 0 when OpenAI fails. During an OpenAI outage, ALL submissions (including spam) pass as legitimate and trigger Slack/Email notifications. This is a deliberate design choice but the risk of spam flooding during outage is undocumented.
findings.suggestion: Document the fail-open trade-off in SECURITY.md. Consider an alternative: queue unscored messages rather than defaulting to non-spam.

findings.description: Google Sheets documentId is 'YOUR_SPREADSHEET_ID'. Every execution of Log to Google Sheets fails silently due to continueOnFail. The workflow responds 200 OK while all data logging is lost. Expected for a template, but the demo strategy requires 'screenshot the Google Sheet with processed entries'.
findings.suggestion: Add a note in workflows/README.md that Google Sheets ID must be configured before running. Consider adding a startup check or using n8n static data as a local fallback.

findings.description: Build Warnings checks for AI, Slack, and Email failures but does NOT check Google Sheets failure. If logging fails, the 'warnings' field says 'None' even though data was lost. Operators have no log trail of Sheets failures.
findings.suggestion: Add a try/catch block in Build Warnings that checks $('Log to Google Sheets').all() for errors. Or add a separate warning path.

findings.description: When response.json() fails (server returns non-JSON), the SyntaxError message ('Unexpected token < in JSON') is shown directly in the error banner via getErrorMessage(). This reveals the server returned HTML instead of JSON.
findings.suggestion: Add a catch case in getErrorMessage for SyntaxError: 'The server returned an unexpected response. Please try again.'

findings.description: Code nodes reference other nodes by string name: $('Send Slack Notification'), $('Normalize Fields'). Renaming a node in the n8n editor breaks these references silently at runtime.
findings.suggestion: This is inherent to n8n. Document in workflows/README.md that node names must not be changed. Add a test that checks Code node jsCode for all referenced node names.

findings.description: validation.js must load before script.js (both use defer). script.js depends on globals getValidationMessage, isSpamResult, getErrorMessage. If load order changes, script.js throws ReferenceError.
findings.suggestion: Acceptable for this project size. For growth: bundle with a build tool or use ES modules with explicit imports.

findings.description: Success response (200) sends full JSON with is_spam, ai_failed fields. Spam response (200) sends a different shape: {spam: true, message, category, spam_score, spam_reason}. Client handles both correctly but the inconsistent shapes make the API harder to document.
findings.suggestion: Consider a unified response envelope: {status: 'success'|'spam'|'error', data: {...}}.

findings.description: OpenAI retryOnFail with maxTries:3 and 30s timeout means worst case 94s (3x30s + 2x2s waits). Client timeout is 30s. If the first attempt fails slowly, the client times out while n8n continues retrying in the background, consuming API credits for attempts the user will never see the result of.
findings.suggestion: Reduce OpenAI timeout to 15s (still generous for gpt-4o) or reduce maxTries to 2. Total worst case would be 32s, closer to client timeout.

planAttacks.attack: Auth token in client JS is security theater - any page visitor can extract it and bypass auth entirely.
planAttacks.defense: It prevents completely unauthenticated requests. For a portfolio demo, it demonstrates auth awareness. Production mitigation is documented in SECURITY.md.

planAttacks.attack: Prompt injection is trivially exploitable - user can override AI classification and inject arbitrary content into Slack/Email notifications.
planAttacks.defense: The response_format: json_object constrains output shape, and Parse AI Response has fallback handling. But the attacker CAN control classification values within the valid schema.

planAttacks.attack: No rate limiting enables cost abuse via automated requests to the OpenAI-backed webhook.
planAttacks.defense: SECURITY.md documents the risk with 3 production mitigation options. The demo runs locally with no public exposure.

planAttacks.attack: OpenAI outage causes fail-open: all submissions default to spam_score 0, enabling spam flooding with full notifications.
planAttacks.defense: The ai_failed flag is set and tracked in warnings. The fallback is intentionally fail-open to avoid blocking legitimate users. Graceful degradation is present.

planAttacks.attack: Notification failures are silent to the user - Slack/Email/Sheets can all fail and the user still sees success.
planAttacks.defense: From the submitter's perspective, their message WAS processed (AI analysis ran, results shown). Notifications are internal operations. Build Warnings tracks failures for operators.
